subject: Geography
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the concept and characteristics of minerals
Analyse the different modes of occurrence of minerals
Compare ferrous and non-ferrous minerals and their uses
Interpret maps showing mineral distribution in India
Evaluate the role of conventional energy sources in industrial development
Examine the potential of renewable energy sources in India
Understand the need for conservation of mineral and energy resources

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Minerals and Their Importance
topics_covered: Role of minerals in daily life
student_activities: Identify minerals used in household objects
Explain how minerals contribute to industries

lesson_plan.title: Modes of Occurrence of Minerals
topics_covered: Minerals in igneous and metamorphic rocks
student_activities: Draw and label diagrams of mineral occurrence
Give examples of minerals found in each formation

lesson_plan.title: Types of Minerals
student_activities: Create a comparison table of mineral types
List major uses of each mineral

lesson_plan.title: Distribution of Minerals in India
topics_covered: Mineral-rich regions such as the Chota Nagpur plateau
student_activities: Mark mineral regions on an outline map of India
Explain why certain regions are rich in minerals

lesson_plan.title: Conventional Energy Resources
teaching_strategy: Discussion on energy use in industries
student_activities: Discuss advantages and disadvantages of fossil fuels

lesson_plan.title: Renewable Energy Sources
teaching_strategy: Visual presentations of renewable energy projects
student_activities: Prepare a chart showing renewable energy sources in India

lesson_plan.title: Conservation of Minerals and Energy
student_activities: Write ways to conserve energy at home and school

teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect mineral and energy resources with real-world economic activities to improve conceptual understanding.
